mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending and overall economic health, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:strong consumer spending supports corporate profits
failure_condition: If RSAFS growth is consistently positive, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of observations over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which can lead to wage inflation and eventually prompt the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates, negatively impacting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:tight labor markets can precede inflation
failure_condition: If UNRATE falls below 5.0% and S&P 500 60-day forward returns are predominantly positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year period, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is accelerating while the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) remains at a low level, it indicates an overly accommodative monetary policy, leading to negative real interest rates that encourage risk-taking and support equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the specified trigger conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of such periods over a 3-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, a sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a broad rebound in manufacturing and economic activity, which is highly bullish for corporate profits and stock prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows sustained growth during a recovery, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) in those periods, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp and sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates weakening construction activity and broader economic deceleration, which foreshadows reduced demand and lower corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls below 800.0 (thousands of units)
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST falls below the specified threshold, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year period, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins strong consumer confidence and spending, thereby supporting corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 performance over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is consistently positive, but S&P 500 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of observations over any 2-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is at its effective lower bound, it signals maximum monetary accommodation, which reduces the cost of capital and encourages risk-taking, thereby providing strong support for equity valuations over a monthly horizon, especially during periods of recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:zero interest rate policy supports asset prices
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% for a sustained period, but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during that regime, this hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of robust GDP growth (GDPC1) and a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a strong and healthy economy, creating an optimal environment for corporate profitability and investor confidence, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If both GDPC1 growth and UNRATE conditions are met, but S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 5-year rolling window, this hypothesis is rejected.
